title: tanarius depressus
intro: Tanarius depressus, also known as the "tansy ragwort," is a perennial herb belonging to the sunflower family, native to Europe and western Asia, and is characterized by its yellow or white flowers and bitter, milky sap. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as a natural antiseptic due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Tanarius depressus include its potential to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, as well as its ability to act as a natural insect repellent. Tanarius depressus is often prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes, although its use is not recommended without proper guidance due to its potential toxicity when ingested in large quantities.
